scope: Tier-2 auth-hook migration: the 4 legacy-parity read streams only (products, plans, customers, subscriptions), each a distinct GraphQL root QUERY field on the SAME single POST /graphql endpoint; Stigg's much larger documented GraphQL surface (mutations, coupons, addons, usage/entitlement measurement types, webhooks) is out of scope until Pass B. Every covered/excluded entry below is declared method GET (not the literal wire HTTP verb, which is always POST for GraphQL) to correctly signal READ semantics to connectorgen validate's capabilities.write/read cross-check (design SS E.1 rule 4) -- Stigg's GraphQL transport uses POST for both queries and mutations, so the wire verb alone cannot distinguish them.

excluded.reason: Pass B capability expansion; stigg is read-only in this migration (legacy Write returns ErrUnsupportedOperation)
